serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy is approximately 100 g, equivalent to one individual packet of the product.

nutrient_source.title: Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy — Nutrition Information Panel (product label data as reported in HPB Singapore Foodo database)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore — Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy (cr_id G00010643). The input per_100g values (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g) are preserved as reported. These values are atypical for a noodle dish and likely reflect the seasoning/sauce component only rather than the full assembled product; this limitation is documented in notes. Fat_g is reported as 0.0 g in the HPB source — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo record. Secondary nutrient values (sodium, saturated fat, sugar, dietary fibre) are estimated from USDA Branded Food closest analogous salted egg noodle seasoning product (USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID 2103862, 'Salted Egg Yolk Seasoning Powder') and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for wheat noodles (cooked, plain), which is the primary carbohydrate substrate. Given the very low carbohydrate content reported (2.36 g/100g) — which likely reflects only the seasoning component — the glycemic load per 100 g serving is negligible at approximately 1.3. If the full assembled noodle dish is considered (~40 g carbohydrate/100g), GI would remain ~55 (medium) with GL ~22 (high). GL calculated as: GI x carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 55 x 2.36 / 100 = 1.3. GI 55 is categorized as 'low' according to University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (GI <= 55 is low).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high estimated sodium content (~850 mg/100g) from salted egg yolk seasoning, salt, and flavour enhancers
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy, as the estimated sodium of approximately 850 mg/100g substantially exceeds the 600 mg/100g threshold for high-sodium foods and can acutely elevate blood pressure; if consumed, restrict to a very small portion and avoid pairing with other sodium-dense foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Reported carbohydrate of 2.36 g/100g likely reflects seasoning component only; full assembled noodle dish would carry significantly higher carbohydrate (~40-60 g/100g) with medium GI (~55)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should verify the complete nutrition label for the full assembled product before consumption, as the reported 2.36 g carbohydrate/100g appears to reflect only the seasoning packet; the full noodle dish would deliver a medium-GI carbohydrate load (~40-60 g/100g) requiring careful portion control and blood glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium from salted egg yolk seasoning and flavour enhancers; ultra-processed product with cholesterol-rich salted egg yolk and potential trans fats from processing
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this ultra-processed salted egg noodle product due to its high estimated sodium (~850 mg/100g), cholesterol-rich salted egg yolk content, and the presence of flavour enhancers (MSG, disodium inosinate) that collectively increase cardiovascular risk; if consumed, limit to an occasional very small portion within a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium, nightshade chilli ingredient, and pro-inflammatory processed flavour enhancers with no anti-inflammatory nutritional offset
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product as the spicy chilli seasoning contains nightshade compounds that may trigger joint inflammation, the high estimated sodium (~850 mg/100g) worsens tissue swelling, and the processed flavour enhancers (MSG, disodium inosinate) may exacerbate systemic inflammation without any compensating anti-inflammatory nutrients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy is a Singaporean ultra-processed packaged noodle product produced by Irvin's, a brand well known for its salted egg yolk snack range. The product consists of wheat noodles coated in a rich, intensely savoury salted egg yolk sauce with added chilli for heat, creating a bold umami-forward flavour profile with a spicy finish. The texture is soft and chewy from the noodles, with a glossy, slightly sticky sauce coating. Salted egg yolk — made from duck eggs cured in brine — provides the characteristic buttery, briny richness that defines the product. The spicy variant adds chilli and pepper seasoning, making it suitable for consumers who enjoy heat alongside the savoury egg flavour. Nutritionally, the HPB-reported values (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100g) are atypical for a noodle dish and are assessed to likely reflect only the seasoning or sauce component rather than the full assembled product; the complete noodle dish would carry substantially higher energy (~400-450 kcal/100g), carbohydrate (~40-60 g/100g), and fat content. Sodium is estimated at approximately 850 mg/100g, placing this product in the high-sodium category. Clinically, this product is of high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium load, and moderate concern for type 2 diabetes if the full noodle carbohydrate content is considered. It is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to nightshade chilli content and pro-inflammatory processing. The product is popular as a lunch or dinner option in Singapore and is widely available in supermarkets and convenience stores across the island.

confidence_reason: The HPB Foodo database is an approved primary source for Singapore foods and provides an exact match for this product (cr_id G00010643). However, the reported per_100g macronutrient values (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g) are nutritionally atypical for a noodle dish and are assessed to likely reflect only the seasoning/sauce component rather than the full assembled product. Secondary nutrients (sodium, sugar, dietary fibre, saturated fat) are estimated from USDA Branded Food analogous data. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high due to the incomplete or component-only nature of the primary nutrient data.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): fat_g is reported as 0.0 g per 100g in the HPB Foodo source for this product entry — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database record (cr_id G00010643). This value is atypical for a salted egg noodle product and likely reflects the seasoning component only rather than the full assembled dish; the full product would be expected to contain fat from salted egg yolk. saturated_fat_g is also 0.0 g, consistent with the fat_g confirmed zero from the HPB source. Users should verify against the complete product nutrition label. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all set to -1. These detailed micronutrient and fatty acid values are not available in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product, and no sufficiently close match was found in USDA FoodData Central for the specific Irvin's Salted Egg Noodle Spicy product as of 2026-04-16. 3. DATA INTEGRITY CAVEAT: The input per_100g values (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g) are preserved as reported from HPB but are assessed to reflect only the seasoning/sauce packet component, not the full noodle dish. The complete assembled product would be expected to have significantly higher energy (~400-450 kcal/100g), carbohydrate (~40-60 g/100g), and fat (~15-25 g/100g). Downstream users should verify against the full product nutrition information panel. 4. SODIUM ESTIMATE: Sodium of 850 mg/100g is estimated from USDA Branded Food analogous salted egg seasoning products and is consistent with known sodium levels in Irvin's salted egg product range; this is not directly reported in the HPB entry and should be verified against the product label. 5. HALAL STATUS: Irvin's halal certification status should be verified directly with the brand; is_halal is set to false pending confirmation. 6. GI NOTE: GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on wheat noodles (cooked) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. GI 55 is categorized as 'low' according to University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (GI <= 55 is low).
